title: Strawberry Shortcake
description: Buttery homemade shortcakes split and layered with macerated strawberries and fresh whipped cream. A summer showstopper.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Prepare strawberries: combine sliced strawberries, sugar, and lemon juice in a bowl. Toss well and let macerate at room temperature for at least 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until juicy.
Make shortcakes: whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Cut cold butter into the flour mixture using a pastry cutter or fingers until it resembles coarse crumbs with pea-sized butter pieces.
Add heavy cream and vanilla. Stir just until the dough comes together — do not overmix.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Pat to ¾-inch thickness. Cut into 6 rounds using a 2.5-inch biscuit cutter.
Place on prepared baking sheet. Brush tops with cream and sprinkle with sugar.
Bake 15–18 minutes until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
Whip cream: beat heavy cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla to soft peaks.

To assemble: split each shortcake horizontally. Spoon macerated strawberries and their juice over the bottom half. Add a generous dollop of whipped cream. Place the top half on and add more strawberries and cream. Serve immediately.

notes: Cold butter is the secret to flaky shortcakes — don't let it soften before cutting it in. The strawberry maceration step is not optional — it creates the sweet, jammy juices that soak into the biscuit.
